title: Player Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Accessibility, and Evidence · Runtime Lens: Accessibility Charter
summary: A studio says “make Asteroids” but has not defined the player, verbs, success, failure, control needs, sensory alternatives, supported devices, repository state, or acceptance evidence. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the accessibility charter increment in player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter. Learners progress through p…
objectives: Record input, vision, hearing, motion, cognition, timing, text, difficulty, and assistive-technology needs before implementation.
Inspect status, diff, revision, ignore rules, environment files, assets, licenses, and existing tests before editing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Asteroids team handles case 8275 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Record input, vision, hearing, motion, cognition, timing, text, difficulty, and assistive-technology needs before implementation. A defensible solution applies Accessibility Charter, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-outcomes-repo-accessibility-2
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-outcomes-repo-accessibility-2.
# Case 7417: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def worked_asteroids_outcomes_repo_accessibility_2(player_goal: str = "survive-and-score", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, playtest_owner: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "playerworked2",
        "seed7417",
        "input2dekrow",
        "frameworked27417",
        "state7417worked2",
        "playtest2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if not player_goal.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-game"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not playtest_owner:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "unowned-evidence"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-game-charter"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Asteroids decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected frame, input, seam, collision, cue, save, package, or release case, then state which native pygame-ce, SDL, display, controller, audio, filesystem, GPU, accessibility playtest, installer, performance, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Accessibility Charter evidence · Theory Accessibility Charter
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-accessibility-charter-1bpe65" that uses case 2768 to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-accessibility-charter-1bpe65
# Competency: asteroids-accessibility-charter.
# Case 2768: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets.
# Changed case: inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death.
def evidence_asteroids_accessibility_charter_1bpe65(player_goal: str = "survive-and-score", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, playtest_owner: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1bpe65 = "1bpe65-56epb1-2768"
    assert evidence_variant_1bpe65
    evidence_axes_1bpe65 = (
        "player1bpe65",
        "seed2768",
        "input56epb1",
        "frame1bpe652768",
        "state27681bpe65",
        "playtest56epb12768",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1bpe65)) == 6
    if not player_goal.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-game"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not playtest_owner:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "unowned-evidence"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-game-charter"

options.text: Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the accessibility charter defect evidence boundary.

For repair the accessibility charter defect, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Accessibility Charter, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Accessibility Charter model · Theory Accessibility Char…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Accessibility Charter: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 7561 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Record input, vision, hearing, motion, cognition, timing, text, difficulty, and assistive-technology needs before implementation.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Accessibility Charter model · Theory Accessibility Char….
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Accessibility Charter model · Theory Accessibility Char…, keep Accessibility Charter connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record input, vision, hearing, motion, cognition, timing, text, difficulty, and assistive-technology needs before implementation.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Accessibility Charter model · Theory Accessibility Char…, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Accessibility Charter precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Accessibility Charter model · Theory Accessibility Char…, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 7561 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Record input, vision, hearing, motion, cognition, timing, text, difficulty, and assistive-technology needs before implementation.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Accessibility Charter model · Theory Accessibility Char…, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Accessibility is a post-release options-menu polish task.
